defects.symptom: dummyindex's own generated files (.claude/skills/dummyindex*/**, .claude/CLAUDE.md, equip-generated .claude/agents/*.md, .claude/commands/*, design artifacts) permanently pollute the reconcile delta: they appear as unassigned_new_files in `reconcile --json`, block `reconcile-stamp` (forcing routine --force), fill the SessionStart drift report, and trigger the Stop reconcile-gate. There is NO ignore/exclusion mechanism for the reconcile path — ignore files only gate the index build. Covers evidence items 0,1,2,6,7,12,15,16,29,37,43,44,48,53 and is the proximate driver of the auto_council flip-flop (item 4). Reproduced live at HEAD: a fresh `.claude/skills/dummyindex/SKILL.md` shows up in unassigned_new_files and the gate blocks naming it.
locations: dummyindex/context/build/reconcile.py:109-111 (unassigned filter is ONLY `not _is_context_path(p)`)
dummyindex/context/build/reconcile.py:242-244 (_is_context_path covers only .context/)
dummyindex/context/build/reconcile.py:174-176 (stamp guard blocks on those same unassigned files)
dummyindex/context/drift.py:104,110-111,142-143 (drift report reuses the unfiltered reconcile signals)
dummyindex/context/reconcile_gate.py:184-186 (gate keys has_drift on the same report)
dummyindex/context/domains/config.py:12-17,83-88 (ContextConfig schema has no exclude/ignore field)
dummyindex/pipeline/io/detect.py:265-268 + dummyindex/pipeline/build/common.py:14 (.dummyindexignore/.gitignore consumed only by the index-build pipeline, never by git_delta/reconcile)
dummyindex/installer/install.py:100-143 (installer writes .claude/skills/dummyindex*/ + .dummyindex_version — the exact files later flagged)
dummyindex/context/domains/equip/lifecycle/manifest.py:1-17 (equipment.json already records every file equip wrote to .claude/)
defects.root_cause: compute_reconcile_report builds unassigned_new_files from raw `git diff`/`git status -uall` added paths with a single hard-coded filter (`_is_context_path`). dummyindex's installer and equip write dozens of files under .claude/ that no feature can ever own, and neither a built-in tool-path exemption nor a config exclude knob nor the existing .dummyindexignore mechanism is consulted, so the tool flags its own install footprint as un-reconciled work forever. Because the stamp guard (reconcile.py:174-176) blocks on the same set, --force becomes the normalized exit, which erodes the anchor's data-loss guarantee (real unassigned files get force-stamped past alongside the noise).
defects.proposed_fix: Single choke-point fix in compute_reconcile_report (covers reconcile CLI, drift report, stamp guard, and Stop gate at once): (1) add `_is_tool_path(path)` next to `_is_context_path` in dummyindex/context/build/reconcile.py excluding `.claude/` and `.claude-design/` prefixes from unassigned_new_files (DESIGN DECISION: exclude all of .claude/ — recommended, since the feature taxonomy should never own agent/skill wiring — vs only dummyindex-managed subpaths `.claude/skills/dummyindex*`, `.claude/CLAUDE.md`, plus paths listed in .context/equipment.json; the broader rule is simpler and matches what every transcript agent manually concluded). (2) Add an optional `reconcile_exclude: tuple[str, ...]` field (fnmatch globs, default ()) to ContextConfig in dummyindex/context/domains/config.py (to_payload/from_payload, tolerant of absence for back-compat) and have compute_reconcile_report load it via the existing config read path and filter added/removed paths through it — gives users the knob for repo-specific noise (docs/spikes/**, supabase/migrations/**, *.png design exports). (3) Update the --force warning and 65-reconcile.md step 4 text to point at `reconcile_exclude` as the sanctioned 'stop tracking it' mechanism. Keep the filter pure (frozen dataclass report unchanged; filtering happens before construction).
defects.tests_to_add: tests/context/build/test_reconcile.py: untracked + committed `.claude/agents/x.md` and `.claude/skills/dummyindex/SKILL.md` do NOT appear in unassigned_new_files and do not refuse the stamp; `reconcile_exclude: ['docs/spikes/**']` in config.json filters a matching added file; non-matching source file still reported. tests/context/test_reconcile_gate.py: gate allows stop when the only added paths are under .claude/. tests/context/domains/test_config.py: round-trip of reconcile_exclude, absent-field back-compat.

defects.symptom: Rebase/squash orphans meta.indexed_commit and the tool gives a tool-backed FALSE ALL-CLEAR: with an anchor SHA unknown to the repo, `reconcile` prints 'anchor deadbeefdead / in sync — nothing to reconcile' and `--json` returns has_drift:false (reproduced live at HEAD). With an orphaned-but-still-present anchor, reconcile silently diffs against the dangling commit and reports phantom drift from other PRs. Recovery requires manual git archaeology and hand-editing meta.json (no rollback/set verb). Covers evidence items 19, 20, 27, 30, 49 and re-resurrection of triaged noise after rebase (44, with D1).
locations: dummyindex/context/build/reconcile.py:95-100 (changed_paths None → empty report with indexed_commit set, has_drift False — indistinguishable from genuinely clean)
dummyindex/context/build/git_delta.py:95-114 (changed_paths returns None for an unknown `since`; no `git cat-file -e` / `merge-base --is-ancestor` probe anywhere in the package)
dummyindex/cli/reconcile.py:151-153 (prints 'in sync — nothing to reconcile' for that empty report)
dummyindex/context/build/reconcile.py:160-195 (stamp_reconciled always writes head_commit; no --to <commit> recovery path)
dummyindex/cli/reconcile.py:54-63 (run_stamp accepts only --force)
defects.root_cause: changed_paths conflates three failure modes into one `None` (git absent / not a repo / anchor SHA unknown), and compute_reconcile_report maps that None to an EMPTY report (reconcile.py:96-100) whose has_drift is False — so a gc'd or never-fetched anchor reads as 'fully reconciled'. When the orphaned anchor object still exists (reflog), `git diff <dangling>` succeeds and attributes the entire divergence-since-fork as drift. No code validates `indexed_commit` is an ancestor of HEAD, no message mentions history rewrite, and the only way to move the anchor anywhere but HEAD is editing meta.json by hand.
defects.proposed_fix: (1) Add two helpers to git_delta.py: `commit_exists(root, sha) -> Optional[bool]` (`git cat-file -e <sha>^{commit}`) and `is_ancestor_of_head(root, sha) -> Optional[bool]` (`git merge-base --is-ancestor <sha> HEAD`), both degrading to None per module convention. (2) Add an `anchor_status` field to ReconcileReport (enum per codebase conventions: OK / MISSING_FROM_REPO / NOT_ANCESTOR / NONE) computed in compute_reconcile_report; when MISSING_FROM_REPO, do NOT return a clean report — keep counts empty but make has_drift True (or add a separate `anchor_broken` property the CLI/gate/drift all check; DESIGN DECISION: overloading has_drift vs a parallel flag — recommend the parallel flag so JSON consumers see exactly why). (3) CLI: replace 'in sync' with an explicit warning for MISSING_FROM_REPO ('anchor <sha> is unknown to this repo — history was rewritten (rebase/squash) or never fetched; re-anchor with `reconcile-stamp --to <commit>` at the last genuinely-reconciled commit, or `reconcile-stamp` after reconciling against HEAD') and print a one-line caution for NOT_ANCESTOR ('anchor is not an ancestor of HEAD — delta may include other branches' work'). (4) Add `reconcile-stamp --to <commit>` that validates the SHA via commit_exists before writing meta (closes the hand-edit-meta.json recovery hole). (5) Document the rebase-recovery procedure in 65-reconcile.md.
defects.tests_to_add: tests/context/build/test_git_delta.py: commit_exists/is_ancestor helpers on real tmp repos (known sha, unknown sha, non-ancestor branch sha). tests/context/build/test_reconcile.py: unknown anchor yields anchor_status MISSING_FROM_REPO and is not reported clean; CLI test asserting the warning text replaces 'in sync'; stamp --to with valid sha writes it, with bogus sha exits 2 and writes nothing.

defects.symptom: Stop reconcile-gate blocks sessions that changed no source (planning-only, git-only, tool-update sessions), conflates drift inherited from earlier sessions with this session's drift ('demands a 40+ agent council' / '14-feature recouncil after git housekeeping'), keeps blocking when the commit-anchored state is fully clean (mtime rows alone drive it), re-blocks on every new user turn despite the help text's 'blocking the session's exit ONCE', and its message demands 'for each drifted feature below, re-run its council enrichment' even when drifted_features is empty. Covers evidence items 1, 4, 6, 8, 9, 10, 21, 23, 36. Reproduced live at HEAD: blocked with only one unassigned .claude file; identical block re-issued on a later stop_hook_active=false stop.
locations: dummyindex/context/reconcile_gate.py:161-198 (decide_block: blocks whenever ANY drift exists AND the session is voluminous — no session attribution)
dummyindex/context/reconcile_gate.py:174 (block-once keyed solely on stop_hook_active; re-arms after any user message)
dummyindex/context/reconcile_gate.py:74-87 (render_block emits the per-feature recouncil directive unconditionally, before checking `if features:`)
dummyindex/context/domains/memory/nudge.py:23,26-30 (is_significant = subagents>0 or output_tokens>=40_000 — pure volume)
dummyindex/context/domains/memory/transcript.py:71-101 (SessionSignal has no edited-source dimension)
dummyindex/context/drift.py:123-137 (mtime rows feed the gate's has_drift even when anchor is at HEAD and reconcile is clean)
dummyindex/cli/help.py:48-57 ('blocking the session's exit ONCE' promise)
dummyindex/cli/reconcile_gate.py:25 (session_id is resolved then discarded — `_session_id`)
defects.root_cause: Three independent gaps confirmed in code: (a) the staleness predicate is global repo drift (mtime rows OR unassigned OR awaiting) with zero attribution to the current session, and significance is volume-only, so any long session in a repo with pre-existing/mtime drift gets gated; (b) 'block once' is implemented only via the stop_hook_active flag, which Claude Code resets on every new user turn — there is no per-session memo (the sibling nudge feature has exactly this memo: nudge.py:33-53 _state_path/already_nudged/mark_nudged — the gate never adopted it despite resolving session_id); (c) render_block's directive text is unconditional, so unassigned-only blocks still instruct council enrichment of nonexistent drifted features.
defects.proposed_fix: (1) Block-once-per-session: persist a gate memo in .context/cache/ keyed by session_id, mirroring nudge.py's _state_path/already_nudged/mark_nudged (cli/reconcile_gate.py already has session_id at line 25); decide_block returns None when already blocked this session. Keep stop_hook_active as the fast first check. (2) Session attribution: extend read_session_signal (or add a sibling reader, staying in the memory.transcript domain) to collect repo-relative file paths from Write/Edit/NotebookEdit tool_use entries; decide_block only blocks when at least one edited path lies outside .context/ and .claude/ — i.e. the session plausibly caused source drift (DESIGN DECISION: stricter variant — intersect edited paths with drifted features' files / unassigned files — is more precise but more brittle; the outside-.context edit test is the recommended minimum). Inherited drift then surfaces via SessionStart report instead of trapping exit. (3) Staleness predicate: when the commit-anchored report is clean (no unassigned, no awaiting, anchor==HEAD) do not block on mtime rows alone — downgrade to silence (the SessionStart report already nudges) (DESIGN DECISION: drop mtime from the gate entirely vs keep for anchor-less repos; recommend keeping mtime-blocking only when no anchor exists). (4) render_block: emit the recouncil sentence only `if features:`, and a placement-only directive ('place the new files, then reconcile-stamp') when only unassigned files exist. Note D1 removes the .claude false-trigger class entirely; this defect remains for real inherited drift.
defects.tests_to_add: tests/context/test_reconcile_gate.py: (a) same session_id blocks once then allows (memo file written under .context/cache/); (b) blocks with stop_hook_active=false after memo present → None; (c) transcript with zero Write/Edit outside .context/.claude → None even with drift; (d) transcript editing app/x.py → blocks; (e) render_block with empty features omits 'drifted feature' directive and includes placement directive; (f) mtime-only drift with anchor==HEAD → None (if variant adopted).

defects.symptom: reconcile-stamp advances the anchor with no content validation: with a pre-anchor index (indexed_commit null) it bootstraps straight to HEAD, silently zeroing the commit-anchored view while 12 features' docs are known-stale (item 35); --force prints a doubly-wrong warning ('It WILL be re-reported next reconcile' — false for committed files, which drop behind the new anchor; 'or stop tracking it' — no such mechanism exists) (items 0, 3, 15); and the no-anchor reconcile output prescribes a DESTRUCTIVE remedy — 'Commit the repo and re-run a fresh `dummyindex ingest`' — which re-clusters and destroys the curated taxonomy (item 34), while also printing 'in sync — nothing to reconcile' for a state it cannot assess. Reproduced live at HEAD.
locations: dummyindex/context/build/reconcile.py:91-93 (null anchor → empty report → stamp guard sees nothing to block on)
dummyindex/context/build/reconcile.py:160-195 (stamp_reconciled: only guard is unassigned/awaiting from the report it is about to invalidate — circular)
dummyindex/cli/reconcile.py:113-122 (--force warning text: 'WILL be re-reported … or stop tracking it')
dummyindex/cli/reconcile.py:144-149 (no-anchor advice: '.. re-run a fresh `dummyindex ingest` to start tracking')
dummyindex/cli/reconcile.py:152-153 ('in sync — nothing to reconcile' printed even when indexed_commit is None)
defects.root_cause: stamp_reconciled validates only against the report computed from the very anchor it rewrites (circular: once stamped past unreconciled commits, every later report reads clean), and the no-anchor path returns an empty report so bootstrap stamping is guard-free with no acknowledgment that pre-existing staleness exits the commit-anchored view forever. The --force warning was written generically: committed unassigned files in fact drop out of `git diff <new-anchor>` immediately (only untracked files and .pending-enrichment markers persist), and 'stop tracking it' references a mechanism the codebase does not provide (no ignore — see D1). The no-anchor message predates the non-destructive bootstrap option (`reconcile-stamp` works fine to start tracking) and contradicts the tool's own never-reclobber doctrine — a memory-less agent following it would wipe curated features.
defects.proposed_fix: (1) Bootstrap acknowledgment: in run_stamp, when the pre-stamp report has indexed_commit None, print an explicit notice ('starting commit-anchored tracking at <sha>; staleness predating this point will NOT appear in commit-anchored signals — review the SessionStart drift report for pre-existing stale features') (DESIGN DECISION: notice-only vs requiring a --bootstrap flag; recommend notice-only to avoid breaking the documented bootstrap-by-stamp flow that 65-reconcile.md fallback relies on). (2) Rewrite the --force warning to be accurate: committed files will drop behind the anchor; only UNTRACKED files and .pending-enrichment markers re-report; replace 'stop tracking it' with a pointer to `reconcile_exclude` once D1 lands (sequence this fix after D1 or land the text in the same change). (3) Replace the destructive no-anchor advice with: 'run `dummyindex context reconcile-stamp` to start commit-anchored tracking (non-destructive; never re-clusters)' and suppress the 'in sync' line when indexed_commit is None (print 'commit-anchored signals unavailable' instead). (4) The circular-validation hole is structurally closed by D2's anchor_status + `--to` verb; no further stamp-side validation is feasible without a second source of truth — note this in the stamp docstring.
defects.tests_to_add: tests/context/build/test_reconcile.py + tests/cli: stamp on a meta with indexed_commit null prints the bootstrap notice; --force output asserts the corrected warning wording (committed vs untracked distinction); no-anchor `reconcile` output contains 'reconcile-stamp' and does NOT contain 'fresh `dummyindex ingest`' or 'in sync'.

defects.symptom: Three contradictory staleness oracles answer 'is .context stale?' differently in the same session — mtime plan-update says '66 files / 12 features', commit-anchored reconcile says 'in sync', rebuild's check says 'no feature drift' — producing tool-backed false all-clears (user asked three times, Claude declared clean, `context check` later revealed 36 added + 26 modified unabsorbed files). The mtime signal mass-false-positives after git pull/rebase/checkout (51→63 phantom files; cleared only by bulk `touch` of every .context doc). Covers evidence items 20, 22, 33, 55.
locations: dummyindex/context/drift.py:123-137 (mtime rows: src.stat().st_mtime > newest doc mtime — git checkout/pull rewrites source mtimes, making content-identical files read as drift)
dummyindex/context/build/reconcile.py:79-119 (oracle 2: commit-anchored delta — blind to anything behind the anchor)
dummyindex/cli/check.py:1-16 + dummyindex/context/build/manifest.py:41-53,186 (oracle 3: sha256 manifest compare — the only content-true signal, but manual-only and consulted by nothing else)
dummyindex/context/drift.py:147-173 (render mixes oracles 1+2 with no precedence note)
defects.root_cause: Confirmed by design-reading all three modules: the mtime heuristic compares raw st_mtime with no content check, so any git operation that rewrites working-tree files (pull/rebase/checkout) regenerates source mtimes newer than doc mtimes and floods the report with phantom rows; the commit-anchored report and the sha256 manifest each measure a different thing; no command cross-references them and no doc states precedence. (Note: the evidence's specific claim of '>= / equal-mtime flags drift' is wrong — drift.py:136 uses strict `>`; the false positives come from git writing source after docs within the same second and from wholesale mtime refresh.)
defects.proposed_fix: Make the mtime signal content-aware by cross-filtering through the existing manifest: in compute_drift, after building candidate rows, drop any row whose file's current sha256 equals its entry in .context/cache/manifest.json (load once via manifest read helpers; tolerate absent manifest = keep current behavior). This kills git-operation false positives at the source with no new state, fixing the gate and SessionStart report together (DESIGN DECISION: hashing every candidate file costs ~ms per file and only runs for already-mtime-stale files, so the set is small; alternative of git-aware mtimes is far more complex — recommend manifest cross-check). Additionally: (a) add a `--json` mode to plan-update emitting which signal produced each entry, and (b) one paragraph in 65-reconcile.md + help.py stating precedence: manifest hashes are content truth, commit anchor tracks reconciliation, mtime is a heuristic nudge.
defects.tests_to_add: tests/context/test_drift.py: source file with mtime newer than docs but sha256 matching manifest produces NO row; same file with changed content produces a row; absent manifest preserves legacy behavior; gate integration test — repo where every mtime is newer post-'checkout' but hashes match → decide_block returns None.

defects.symptom: `reconcile --json` returns has_drift:true while drifted/unassigned/awaiting are all zero, driven by uncommitted deletions of `.context/`'s OWN files (pruned flow docs) in removed_files — so finishing a reconcile necessarily leaves has_drift true until the commit, contradicting 65-reconcile.md's explicit claim that 'Drift detection filters `.context/` paths everywhere'. Evidence item 24. Reproduced live at HEAD: deleting .context/flows/f.md yields removed_files=['.context/flows/f.md'], has_drift:true.
locations: dummyindex/context/build/reconcile.py:116 (removed_files=tuple(sorted(delta.removed)) — no _is_context_path filter, unlike unassigned at :109-111)
dummyindex/context/build/reconcile.py:104-107 (modified/removed → owners drift mapping also unfiltered, currently harmless since features never own .context paths)
dummyindex/skills/council/65-reconcile.md:172-175 (doc claims .context-only commits never self-drift — currently false for deletions)
defects.root_cause: The `_is_context_path` exclusion was applied only to the `unassigned` branch of compute_reconcile_report; `delta.removed` flows into removed_files (and the has_drift OR at reconcile.py:70-76) unfiltered, so the reconcile's own .context cleanup re-reports as drift.
defects.proposed_fix: In compute_reconcile_report, filter `_is_context_path` out of delta.removed before building removed_files, and symmetrically out of the modified/removed paths fed to the owners-drift loop (one-line guard in the loop at :105). Doc claim then becomes true as written.
defects.tests_to_add: tests/context/build/test_reconcile.py: uncommitted deletion of a committed .context/flows/x.md yields removed_files=() and has_drift False; deletion of an owned source file still reports (regression guard for test_removed_owned_file_drifts_feature_and_lists_removal).

defects.symptom: `context hooks install` printed 'skipped (already current): claude/SessionStart, claude/Stop, claude/PreCompact' while silently rewriting .claude/settings.json — it added the brand-new reconcile-gate command to the Stop entry; the agent only caught the change via git diff. Evidence item 38.
locations: dummyindex/context/claude_settings.py:85-93 (install_hook_entry: when an existing sentinel entry's body differs it replaces it in place, write_settings, then returns False — same value as 'already current')
dummyindex/cli/hooks.py:57-61 (maps False to the 'skipped (already current)' line)
defects.root_cause: install_hook_entry collapses three outcomes (appended / refreshed-in-place / already-identical) into a bool; the CLI reports the False branch as 'already current' even when the file was just modified.
defects.proposed_fix: Replace the bool with a tri-state enum per codebase conventions (e.g. `class HookInstallOutcome(str, Enum): INSERTED, REFRESHED, CURRENT` in claude_settings.py); context/hooks.py install() accumulates refreshed separately (extend the frozen HookResult dataclass with `refreshed: tuple[str, ...] = ()` — defaulted, so existing constructions stay valid); cli/hooks.py prints 'hooks install: refreshed (body updated): …' for that bucket. Audit the two call sites of install_hook_entry for the changed return type.
defects.tests_to_add: tests/context/test_claude_settings.py: differing-body sentinel entry returns REFRESHED and writes; identical body returns CURRENT and does not rewrite the file (assert mtime/content); tests/context/test_hooks.py + cli test asserting the 'refreshed' output line when a hook body changes between versions.

defects.symptom: The council resumption skip-signal `features/<id>/.hash` has NO write/check verb in the CLI — council/19-resume.md instructs 'write features/<id>/.hash' as a bash sha256 aggregate, forcing the orchestrating agent to reimplement dummyindex's hashing algorithm in inline python (evidence item 14); and the placement verbs (assign-files/unassign-files) change a feature's file set without invalidating its stale .hash (item 13). Any algorithm drift between skill prose and agent improvisation silently breaks staleness detection.
locations: dummyindex/skills/council/19-resume.md:51-58 (the only specification of the algorithm — prose + pseudo-bash; no package code reads or writes features/<id>/.hash: repo-wide grep finds .hash only in equip lifecycle hashing, an unrelated mechanism)
dummyindex/context/domains/features/placement.py:128-190,193-265 (assign_files/unassign_files mutate payload['files'] and drop the marker but never touch .hash)
defects.root_cause: The .hash write side was specified only in skill prose and never implemented as a CLI verb; the deterministic placement ops that change a feature's file membership have no knowledge of the file, so the skip-signal's integrity rests entirely on each orchestrating agent re-deriving the same sha256-aggregate by hand.
defects.proposed_fix: Add a `context feature-hash` verb (new ContextSubcommand + cli module per one-verb-per-mutation convention) with `--feature <id> --write` (recompute sha256 of sorted per-file sha256s over the feature's files and write features/<id>/.hash) and `--check` (recompute, compare, exit 0/1, print MATCH/MISMATCH) — implementation in the features domain (e.g. dummyindex/context/domains/features/ops.py or a new hashing helper) so the algorithm has exactly one home; update 19-resume.md to call the verb instead of prose-bash. Also have assign_files/unassign_files unlink a stale features/<id>/.hash (invalidation is safe: missing hash → re-council, the conservative direction). DESIGN DECISION: whether mark-enriched should auto---write the hash (convenient but couples two verbs; recommend keeping them separate and letting the skill call feature-hash --write after enrichment).
defects.tests_to_add: tests/context/domains/ (features): feature-hash --write produces a stable digest for fixed file contents; --check exits 1 after a member file edit; assign_files/unassign_files remove an existing .hash; cli registration test (verb appears in help, exits 2 on missing --feature).

defects.symptom: Reconcile choreography friction: the stamp refusal text says 'Reconcile them (place files, enrich features)' without naming `mark-enriched`, so agents on older installs deleted .pending-enrichment markers with rm -f (item 17); unassign-files re-drops the marker on an already-enriched feature when pruning happens after enrichment (item 13b); and mark-enriched clears the marker while the drift report stays fully dirty until the stamp, making the 5-step loop easy to abandon half-done (item 31).
locations: dummyindex/cli/reconcile.py:85-89 (refusal remedy text omits mark-enriched)
dummyindex/context/domains/features/placement.py:248 (unassign_files unconditionally re-drops the marker — documented as intentional at :206-208)
dummyindex/skills/council/65-reconcile.md:94-133 (step order — prune 2.5 before enrich 3 — avoids the re-drop, but nothing enforces or detects out-of-order execution)
defects.root_cause: The marker re-drop is by design (scope change owes re-enrichment) and the verb exists and is named by the marker file itself (placement.py:385-387) and by the drift report (drift.py:251) — the residual defect is purely guidance: the stamp refusal (the moment an agent is actually stuck) doesn't name the two commands that resolve each blocker category, and nothing warns when pruning re-marks a feature enriched seconds earlier.
defects.proposed_fix: (1) Extend _print_blockers/refusal text in cli/reconcile.py to name the resolving verb per category: unassigned → 'assign-files / scaffold-feature', awaiting → 'enrich, then `dummyindex context mark-enriched --feature <id>`'. (2) Have unassign_files print (via its CLI wrapper's result rendering, keeping the domain pure) a note when it re-drops a marker over a feature that had none ('re-enrichment now owed — run mark-enriched after re-enriching'). (3) DESIGN DECISION (optional, larger): a `reconcile-finish` composite verb that runs mark-enriched for explicitly listed features + stamp in one call — recommend deferring; the messaging fixes address the observed failures.
defects.tests_to_add: tests/context/build/test_reconcile.py / cli tests: refusal stderr names mark-enriched and assign-files; unassign CLI output includes the re-enrichment note when the marker was re-dropped.

defects.symptom: SessionStart drift report miscounts: '4 source files across 4 features' when only 2 distinct files changed (one shared file fans out to one row per owning feature and rows are counted, not files) — items 40, 45; and three CLI outputs still recommend BARE `/dummyindex --recouncil`, which 65-reconcile.md:132-133 explicitly forbids ('Always pass the feature id. Bare --recouncil re-runs the whole repo — the opposite of a scoped reconcile') — item 18.
locations: dummyindex/context/drift.py:195-196 (file_count = len(report.rows) counts (feature,file) pairs)
dummyindex/cli/rebuild.py:109 (bare '/dummyindex --recouncil' hint)
dummyindex/cli/reconcile.py:160 (bare hint)
dummyindex/context/drift.py:169 (bare hint in the rendered drift report)
defects.root_cause: _render_mtime_section computes file_count from the row list, where a multi-owner file contributes one row per feature; the hint strings predate / contradict the scoped-recouncil doctrine the skill now teaches.
defects.proposed_fix: file_count = len({r.rel_path for r in report.rows}) in drift.py:195. Change the three hints to the scoped form: '/dummyindex --recouncil <feature-id>' (or 'run the reconcile procedure — council/65-reconcile.md' where no feature id is in scope, as in rebuild's generic message).
defects.tests_to_add: tests/context/test_drift.py: one file owned by 3 features renders '1 source file across 3 features'; string assertions that rendered hints contain '<feature' or the procedure pointer, not the bare command.

defects.symptom: Installed hook commands are labeled `# DUMMYINDEX_AUTO_REFRESH` though they only report drift / nudge / gate (misleads anyone auditing settings.json), and every hook fails open (`command -v dummyindex || exit 0` + `2>/dev/null || true`) so a PATH-broken CLI silently disables drift reporting, the gate, and the nudge indefinitely with no signal. Items 32, 40, 46.
locations: dummyindex/context/hooks.py:41-46 (SENTINEL = 'DUMMYINDEX_AUTO_REFRESH', comment acknowledges it is legacy, kept for upgrade-scrub recognition)
dummyindex/context/hooks.py:63-132 (all five hook command bodies: command -v guard + full stderr suppression)
defects.root_cause: Sentinel string is load-bearing for recognizing/scrubbing entries written by every prior version, so it was frozen at its legacy name; the fail-open guard is deliberate (a hook must never fail the turn) but has no degraded-mode signal whatsoever.
defects.proposed_fix: (1) Rename visibly without breaking recognition: write new entries with a comment line `# DUMMYINDEX_HOOKS (managed by dummyindex; reports drift / gates reconcile / nudges memory)` while KEEPING the SENTINEL substring on the same or next line for matcher compatibility (recognition is `SENTINEL in command`, so adding text is safe — confirmed at hooks.py:439-443). (2) Degraded-mode signal for the SessionStart hook only (the one whose stdout reaches the session): change its guard to `command -v dummyindex >/dev/null 2>&1 || { echo 'dummyindex CLI not found on PATH — drift reporting disabled'; exit 0; }` so a broken install surfaces once per session without failing anything. Keep Stop/PreCompact fully silent (their stdout has protocol meaning). DESIGN DECISION: whether to emit the degraded notice at all vs staying silent — recommend yes for SessionStart only.
defects.tests_to_add: tests/context/test_hooks.py: new install bodies contain both the new comment and the legacy sentinel; uninstall/scrub still removes entries written with old-style bodies (fixture with the old command string); SessionStart body contains the degraded-mode echo.

not_confirmed: Item 17's core claim that no mark-enriched verb exists / is undiscoverable: FIXED at HEAD — the verb is registered (dummyindex/cli/__init__.py:70 ContextSubcommand.MARK_ENRICHED), the .pending-enrichment marker file itself names the exact command (placement.py:385-387), and the drift report's awaiting section names it (drift.py:251). The observed session ran an older installed CLI. Residual messaging gap (stamp refusal text) is covered in the choreography defect.

Item 22's specific mechanism claim ('the heuristic flags equal-or-newer source as drift', i.e. >= semantics): the code uses strict `>` (dummyindex/context/drift.py:136). The mass false positives after git operations are real and confirmed, but they stem from git rewriting working-tree mtimes (sub-second ordering / wholesale refresh), not from equal-mtime comparison — covered in the contradictory-oracles defect with the manifest cross-check fix.

Item 11 (force-stamp anchors to HEAD while built code is uncommitted): works as designed with an explicit warning already in place (build/reconcile.py:194 dirty_source + cli/reconcile.py:123-128). The Model-B anchor structurally cannot represent a working-tree state; noted as a design limitation inside the stamp defect, not a separate bug.

Item 28 (drift hook reported '103 source files across 92 features' after install re-cluster): the drift module faithfully reported the on-disk features/ tree; the 92 shattered community-N dirs are the artifact of the destructive re-cluster — root cause belongs to cluster C1-destructive-rebuild, not the drift signal.

Item 5 (stale retired hook-generation scripts littering user repos): the leftover files live in user repos, written by older installer generations; cleanup-on-upgrade is C6-installer-versions territory.
Items 26 and 54 (build-loop checklist/closing-step says rebuild-only or contradicts SKILL.md): build-loop closing guidance is C7-build-routing territory.
Item 25 (reality-check 100% false-positive contradictions): reality-check claim resolution is C8-council-quality territory.
Item 41 (as-built feature doc stuck at confidence INFERRED): council confidence lifecycle — C8 territory.
Items 42 and 52 (equip-generated prettier/ruff PostToolUse hooks misbehaving): equip-generated tooling — C2-equip-manifest territory.

Items 47 and 50 (reconcile/equip outputs chronically left uncommitted): workflow/docs gap; partially addressed at HEAD by 0c19168 — the gate block message and 65-reconcile.md step 5 now mandate the dedicated `docs(context): reconcile` commit. Remaining enforcement ideas belong with the skill docs, not this cluster's code.

Item 51 (per-repo wiring predates v0.23 gate, never refreshed after CLI upgrade): hook-wiring refresh on upgrade is C6-installer-versions territory (the /dummyindex-update skill at HEAD re-runs install, which refreshes bodies — see the hooks-install reporting defect for the visibility bug that hid exactly this).

Item 39 (memory-nudge fires on read-only audit session): session-memory nudge heuristic, not the reconcile gate; same volume-only is_significant root as the gate defect but the nudge fix belongs to the session-memory cluster.
